instructions.instruction: Use the Tree API exclusively for filesystem operations in generators — never use fs.writeFileSync or direct filesystem writes that bypass dry-run
instructions.original_snippets: Use Tree API exclusively for filesystem operations in generators. BAD: writeFileSync('libs/my-lib/src/index.ts', content). GOOD: tree.write('libs/my-lib/src/index.ts', content).
instructions.relevant_when: implementing a generator that creates or modifies files

instructions.instruction: Derive project paths from readProjectConfiguration(tree, name).root — never hardcode paths like 'libs/my-lib'
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER hardcode project paths in generator logic. BAD: fixed 'libs/my-lib/...' writes. GOOD: derive paths from readProjectConfiguration(tree, name).root.
instructions.relevant_when: writing any file path in a generator

instructions.instruction: Define typed schema.json with required fields, types, defaults, and descriptions — never use schema: any
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER skip schema validation and typed options. BAD: schema: any and no guardrails. GOOD: typed schema.json with required fields, types, defaults, descriptions.
instructions.relevant_when: creating a new generator or executor

instructions.instruction: Read then mutate then update project configuration — never overwrite the full project.json blindly
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER mutate project configuration blindly. BAD: overwrite full project.json. GOOD: const config = readProjectConfiguration(tree, name); config.targets.build = {...}; updateProjectConfiguration(tree, name, config);
instructions.relevant_when: modifying project targets or configuration in a generator

instructions.instruction: Executors must return { success: boolean } and use package notation for executor references in targets
instructions.original_snippets: MUST return { success: boolean } from executor function. MUST use package notation for executor references. BAD: '../../tools/executors:task'. GOOD: '@scope/tools:task'.
instructions.relevant_when: implementing an executor function or registering it in project.json

instructions.instruction: Declare outputs in target configuration for cacheable tasks — never rely on implicit file writes without output declarations
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER omit outputs and dependencies semantics for cacheable work. BAD: no outputs declared, implicit file writes. GOOD: declare outputs in target config: 'outputs': ['{options.outputPath}'].
instructions.relevant_when: registering an executor target that produces build artifacts

instructions.instruction: Call formatFiles(tree) after generateFiles() to ensure generated code has consistent formatting
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER skip formatFiles after generating code. BAD: generateFiles() without calling formatFiles(tree). GOOD: await generateFiles(...); await formatFiles(tree);
instructions.relevant_when: generating source files in a generator

instructions.instruction: Check tree.exists(path) before writing to avoid overwriting existing files
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER ignore existing project structure when generating files. BAD: tree.write(path, content) without checking tree.exists(path). GOOD: if (tree.exists(path)) { throw new Error('File already exists'); }
instructions.relevant_when: writing files in a generator that might already exist

instructions.instruction: Export generator and executor functions from src/index.ts and register them in generators.json/executors.json
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER forget to export generator/executor from plugin index. BAD: generator implemented but missing from src/index.ts and generators.json. GOOD: export in src/index.ts and register in generators.json with factory path.
instructions.relevant_when: completing a generator or executor implementation
